title: styphelia inframediana
intro: Styphelia inframediana is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, commonly known as the mountain pepper plant. The leaves and berries of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Australian Indigenous cultures, and are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Styphelia inframediana has been found to have potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, while the berries can be used to make jams and sauces, and the plant's essential oil can be extracted for use in aromatherapy.
